published_prior_art.cite: CinelliHazlett2020/ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019/AdjahoChristensen2025 supply the component sensitivity geometries; the combined score-level display is bookkeeping

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: sharp joint-misspecification policy-value interval and non-rectangularity witness, not a scalar calibration frontier

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest verified comparators are DiegertMastenPoirier2025, MastenPoirierRen2025, HuangPimentel2025, KallusZhou2021, JinXuZhengZhou2025, and AdjahoChristensen2025

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: non-rectangular joint-misspecification witness, not CH/MSM/covariate-shift penalty separation

published_prior_art.cite: classical L2-vs-centered-L1/projection separation geometry; no checklist paper states the open-set policy-score witness

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal calls tau_Pi* an equivalence frontier between JinXuZhengZhou2025 and AdjahoChristensen2025, but the formal statement only matches local support penalties/rankings after an imposed normalization, not the published criteria as full identified-set or estimator-equivalence objects; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless this is lifted to a named-criterion equivalence frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The phrase that DiegertMastenPoirier2025 and HuangPimentel2025 'license' treating scalar CH or variance-based R2 values as enough for MSM/covariate-shift policy rankings overstates those papers; they compare sensitivity parameters/models, not cross-paper policy-ranking translation.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem statement invokes a 'variance-based R2 ranking' although the formal setup defines only B_C, B_M, and B_S; either add the variance-based weighting language as a fourth formal penalty or remove it from the claim.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Promote the frontier from equality of support-penalty vectors to equality/non-equivalence of the actual robust welfare or identified-set objects in JinXuZhengZhou2025 and AdjahoChristensen2025, with the threshold computed from observables under their published normalizations.
Either formalize Huang-Pimentel variance-based weighting sensitivity as a fourth column in Q_Pi* or delete all 'variance-based R2 ranking' language from Conjecture 1 and the abstract.
Replace the 'licensed by' wording in Conjecture 2 with a neutral comparison: those papers motivate scalar sensitivity comparisons but do not endorse translating scalar R2 values into policy rankings.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1 so the flagship claim is a genuine named-criterion equivalence frontier, and patch the undefined variance-based R2 language.
